title: Arguments given to functions should be of an expected type
description: This rule checks that arguments passed to functions are of the expected type.
recommendation: Use type hints and assert statements to ensure arguments are of the expected type.

noncompliant.description: Argument is an integer when function expects a string.

compliant.description: Argument is a string as expected.

why_is_this_an_issue: Inconsistent function argument types can lead to runtime errors and make the code harder to understand and maintain.
how_to_fix: Use type hints and assert statements to ensure arguments are of the expected type.
